evals.prompt: Train a PPO agent on the CartPole-v1 Gymnasium environment for 10k timesteps, then save the model and evaluate it over 10 episodes reporting mean and std reward.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-stable-baselines3: creates the env with gym.make('CartPole-v1'), instantiates PPO('MlpPolicy', env), calls model.learn(total_timesteps=10000), saves with model.save, and evaluates using evaluate_policy(model, env, n_eval_episodes=10, deterministic=True) reporting mean_reward, std_reward.

assertions.value: Uses the SB3 PPO class with MlpPolicy and model.learn, and evaluates with stable_baselines3.common.evaluation.evaluate_policy rather than a hand-rolled rollout loop.

evals.prompt: I'm doing continuous control on a custom robot reaching task. Use a sample-efficient off-policy algorithm, evaluate periodically and save the best model, and stop early once it hits a reward threshold.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-stable-baselines3: recommends SAC (or TD3) for sample-efficient continuous off-policy control, sets up an EvalCallback that saves the best model, and chains a StopTrainingOnRewardThreshold callback via CallbackList passed to model.learn.

assertions.value: Chooses an off-policy algorithm (SAC or TD3) for continuous control and wires EvalCallback plus StopTrainingOnRewardThreshold through CallbackList.

evals.prompt: I wrote a custom Gymnasium environment for an inventory-management problem. Validate that it conforms to the API before training, then train an agent on it.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-stable-baselines3: runs check_env(env, warn=True) from stable_baselines3.common.env_checker to validate the gymnasium.Env (reset/step signatures, spaces), then trains an SB3 agent (e.g. PPO) on the validated environment.

assertions.value: Validates the custom environment with check_env before training and only then instantiates and trains an SB3 algorithm.

evals.prompt: My training is slow. Spin up several CartPole environments in parallel processes to speed up PPO training and bump the timesteps to 25k.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-stable-baselines3: uses make_vec_env('CartPole-v1', n_envs=4, vec_env_cls=SubprocVecEnv) to parallelize across processes, then trains PPO on the vectorized env with model.learn(total_timesteps=25000). Notes SubprocVecEnv suits compute-heavy/parallel work.

assertions.value: Parallelizes with make_vec_env and SubprocVecEnv rather than a single env, and trains PPO on the vectorized environment.

evals.prompt: I need massively parallel, high-throughput RL training across thousands of vectorized environment instances for a multi-agent setup - throughput is the priority.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT invoke alterlab-stable-baselines3. High-performance massively-parallel and multi-agent RL training is explicitly out of SB3's scope; SB3 itself recommends pufferlib for this. Should defer to alterlab-pufferlib.
